item.title: 'World cup pubs boost' and 'Kate holds court'
item.description: Late openings of pubs and Catherine appearing at Wimbledon lead Friday's front pages.

item.title: How the American Dream has survived 250 years, but only just
item.description: The American Dream has persisted since the days of the Founding Fathers, but faith in the ideal is fading

item.title: Photos: Fans gather in the Bay Area for the World Cup match between the U.S. and Bosnia-Herzegovina
item.description: World Cup watch parties in Santa Clara and the San Francisco Bay Area brought fans together for the United States vs. Bosnia-Herzegovina match, where the U.S. earned a 2-0 win to reach the Round of 16

item.title: Rare copy of US Declaration of Independence found by volunteer in UK archives
item.description: The newly discovered copy is one of only 11 of its kind in the world.

item.title: Weekly quiz: Why did Catherine climb the UK's three highest peaks?
item.description: How much attention did you pay to what happened in the world over the past seven days?

item.title: Chancellor's message to Germany's World Cup exit backfires
item.description: "You've inspired us," read a post by Chancellor Merz on X right after Germany's lackluster performance and early elimination from the FIFA World Cup. It is seen as a communication blunder that has rep

item.title: Cruise ship docked in San Francisco hit by norovirus outbreak
item.description: A Princess Cruises cruise ship docked in San Francisco has seen an outbreak of norovirus, according to the U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention. The CDC said that 102 out of the over 3,000

item.title: Relief efforts shift as Venezuela quake survivors seek aid
item.description: Emergency relief is becoming the focus in Venezuela’s La Guaira state as aid stations distribute food.

item.title: Newly formed Tropical Storm Douglas poses no threat to land, hurricane center says
item.description: Douglas was located about 1,220 miles west-southwest of the southern tip of Mexico's Baja California, the National Hurricane Center said.

item.title: Don't expect trackers to save your stolen car, experts say
item.description: Kia told the BBC UK law prevented its location tracking function being used to live track vehicles.

item.title: Apple butter, sightseeing and puppets: How UK is marking US independence milestone
item.description: As America turns 250 years old, people across the pond are running events of their own to mark the big breakup.

item.title: As Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce prepare to tie the knot, we look back at their relationship
item.description: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce are expected to celebrate their wedding with a party at New York's Madison Square Garden this weekend.

item.title: Diesel sees biggest monthly fall in 26 years. What's happening to fuel prices?
item.description: When the US-Israel war with Iran began, fuel costs jumped, but they are now plummeting as hope of lasting peace grows.

item.title: 'Kane to the rescue' and 'defence black hole triples'
item.description: England Captain Harry Kane's World Cup goals against DR Congo dominate Thursday's front pages.

item.title: US judge sides with NAACP over proposed mail-in ballot restrictions
item.description: President Trump has sought to limit mail-in voting and has ordered his administration to impose limits on the practice.

item.title: Iran football team welcomed home after World Cup exit
item.description: Iran’s football team has landed back in Tehran after their World Cup

item.title: Belgium stage 3–2 comeback win over Senegal to enter World Cup last 16
item.description: Tielemans's penalty late into stoppage time of extra time capped Belgium's comeback from 2-0 down in Seattle.

item.title: USA World Cup star Folarin Balogun receives controversial red card during Round of 32 match
item.description: U.S. men’s national team star Folarin Balogun received a red card in the second half of their Round of 32 World Cup matchup against Bosnia and Herzegovina on Wednesday night.

item.title: Single Injection Reverses Osteoarthritis in Animals in Just 4 Weeks
item.description: It encouraged cartilage to repair itself. ScienceAlert stories are written, fact-checked, and edited by humans, never generated by AI. Don't miss a story, subscribe here.

item.title: Ocean temperatures hit record highs as El Niño looms
item.description: The world’s oceans are under heat stress, with average sea surface temperatures hitting 21°C.
